green_suite.command: PYTHONPATH=src taskset -c 0-3 python -m pytest tests/ -q  (GPU VISIBLE, NOT forced CPU-only)
green_suite.note_no_segv: The full GPU-visible suite ran to completion across all 1256 tests with NO SIGSEGV abort. tests/savepoint/test_dycore_100_steps.py produced an ordinary KeyError (missing WRF reference data), not the CPU-only native crash. This satisfies the contract expectation that the libjax_common.so SEGV should NOT occur in the GPU env.

failure_classification.method: Ran the EXACT 39 failing test files (minus the 2 fixed namelist tests, minus the SEGV-on-CPU savepoint test) on the BASE 7b7c26e with GPU visible. Base produced 86 failures; merged-trunk (same scope) produced 86 failures; the failure SETS are byte-identical (diff empty). Therefore the 86 are pre-existing on the base, NOT merge-induced. The 1 savepoint test + the 2 namelist tests are accounted for separately below.

categories.root_cause: physics-consolidation (namelist-compat) added tests asserting diff_opt=1/km_opt=4 must raise UnsupportedNamelistOption; diffopt1-smagorinsky then WIRED diff_opt=1/km_opt=4 (real 2-D Smagorinsky, parity proofs/v090/diffopt1_smagorinsky_parity.json). Two parallel branches against same base; merge surfaced the stale assertions. PRODUCTION CODE CORRECT.
categories.fix: Updated both tests to assert diff_opt=1/km_opt=4 validates cleanly (and the full pristine em_real oracle namelist is now consumable); km_opt=99 still correctly fails closed. Commit 5670a0e. test_namelist_check.py now 26/26 PASS.

categories.root_cause: M2 backend-bakeoff edge tests spawn subprocesses inside scratch venvs (data/scratch/m2-*-venv) lacking jax/zarr/cupy/triton; ArgInfo TypeErrors are CuPy/Triton kernel-introspection (ADR-001-superseded backends not provisioned here). Identical on base.

FileNotFoundError / CalledProcessError / KeyError downstream of absent fixtures: data/profiler_artifacts/*, data/canairy_meteo/runs/wrf_l3/*, Fortran oracle binaries, wrf_pristine reference trees, publication-audit citation files -- none provisioned in this worktree. Identical on base.

m6b*/m6x* comparator scripts hit 'AttributeError: NoneType has no attribute shape' (theta=None) and assert/KeyError in the comparator-helper / synthetic-dryrun path. These are validation-mode COMPARATOR HELPERS, explicitly 'not the production dycore path'. The production dycore files (dynamics/mu_t_advance.py, core/acoustic.py, core/dycore.py) and the comparator scripts (scripts/m6b5_*, scripts/m6b6_*) are UNCHANGED by all 7 merges (git diff 7b7c26e..HEAD confirms). Identical failure set on base.

Documented FLAKY_ENV_NATIVE_JAX_CRASH (libjax_common.so) plus missing real WRF reference savepoints. In the full GPU-visible suite it produced an ordinary KeyError ('results'), NOT a SEGV, and did NOT abort the suite. In isolation it SEGVs on both base and merged. Listed as KNOWN-ACCEPTABLE in the sprint contract.

baseline_comparison.diff: empty (diff of sorted FAILED lists shows no difference)
baseline_comparison.conclusion: Zero merge regression. Every failure in this scope is pre-existing on the v0.6.0-closed base.

operational_scheme_matrix.gf_cu3_detail: cu=3 -> gf_adapter in CU_SCAN_ADAPTERS; gfdrv_batched imported from physics/_gf_jax; registry GF(3)='implemented'; ACCEPTED_CU_PHYSICS includes 3; physics_dispatch no longer fail-closes GF. (Note: the multicfg_smoke_report.json 'excluded_documented_todo' list still carries a STALE pre-gf-scanwire line calling GF a GPU-batching TODO; the authoritative covered_by_run_configs list and the live code both include cu3-GF. Stale proof-artifact line, not a code issue; left unrewritten to avoid fabricating a proof run.)

wrf_faithful: yes -- no slop: no tolerance loosened, no fix weakened, no synthetic happy-path. The only test change updated 2 stale assertions to match a genuinely-implemented feature (diff_opt=1 Smagorinsky, parity-proven). All 86 environment residuals proven identical to the base.
risks: 87 of 89 suite failures are environment/fixture-dependent (missing optional deps + missing data/oracle/corpus fixtures + a pre-existing m6b*/m6x* comparator-helper theta=None bug). They are NOT merge regressions (base-identical) but they DO mean this deployment cannot run the full historical M2-M7 milestone test suite green. The validation+benchmark burst should run on the provisioned corpus host (or the M2/M5/M6 oracle/venv fixtures must be re-provisioned) for those rows.
The pre-existing m6b*/m6x* comparator-helper bug (theta=None) is in VALIDATION-MODE comparator scripts, not the production dycore; it pre-dates this lane and is a separate carry-over to triage.

test_dycore_100_steps.py SEGVs under CPU-only / in isolation (libjax_common.so native crash) -- run that savepoint tier GPU-visible within the warm full suite, where it degrades to a benign KeyError rather than aborting.
